deepseek_explanations.bacterial infection: Echinacea is an herbal supplement with no proven antibacterial effect; it is not a recommended treatment. The other three are appropriate antibiotics, but the inclusion of an ineffective non-drug makes the overall regimen inappropriate.
deepseek_explanations.intra-abdominal infection: Same as #1; Echinacea is not appropriate for bacterial infections. Amikacin + cefotaxime could be used in severe infections but loracarbef is rarely used for intra-abdominal infections.
deepseek_explanations.neuropathic pain: All four are NSAIDs, which are not first-line for neuropathic pain; neuropathic pain typically requires gabapentinoids, TCAs, or SNRIs. NSAIDs are generally ineffective for pure neuropathic pain.
deepseek_explanations.cluster headache: None of these are standard acute or preventive treatments for cluster headache (acute: oxygen, triptans; preventive: verapamil, lithium). Cyclobenzaprine is a muscle relaxant, lisinopril is for hypertension, and the NSAIDs are not effective for cluster headache.
deepseek_explanations.primary dysmenorrhea: All four are NSAIDs, and NSAIDs (especially naproxen, mefenamic acid) are first-line treatment for primary dysmenorrhea.
deepseek_explanations.musculoskeletal pain: All are NSAIDs, which are appropriate for acute or chronic musculoskeletal pain.
deepseek_explanations.nausea and vomiting: Metoclopramide (prokinetic/antiemetic), dexamethasone (for chemotherapy/post-op nausea), and meclizine (motion sickness) are antiemetics. Lorazepam is used for anticipatory nausea/vomiting but not first-line for most causes.
deepseek_explanations.edema associated with heart failure: Bumetanide (loop diuretic) directly treats edema. ACE inhibitors treat HF but are not primarily for edema. Amlodipine is a CCB not recommended for HF with reduced EF; it may worsen edema.
deepseek_explanations.constipation: Only magnesium hydroxide is an osmotic laxative. Lansoprazole, rabeprazole, omeprazole are PPIs for acid reflux—they do not treat constipation and may even worsen it.
deepseek_explanations.skin and soft tissue infection: Echinacea is inappropriate. Amikacin and cefotaxime are broad-spectrum but not typical first-line for uncomplicated SSTI. Loracarbef is outdated.
deepseek_explanations.angina pectoris: Metoprolol (beta-blocker) is appropriate. Perindopril (ACE inhibitor) is beneficial post-MI but not primarily antianginal. Nicardipine may cause reflex tachycardia. Pindolol has intrinsic sympathomimetic activity and is not preferred for angina.
deepseek_explanations.acne vulgaris: Erythromycin (topical/oral antibiotic), spironolactone (for hormonal acne in females), tazarotene (topical retinoid), oxytetracycline (oral tetracycline). All are recognized acne treatments.
deepseek_explanations.type 2 diabetes mellitus: Canagliflozin and empagliflozin (SGLT2 inhibitors) are appropriate; glimepiride (sulfonylurea) is appropriate; colesevelam (bile acid sequestrant) is FDA-approved for type 2 diabetes.
deepseek_explanations.ulcerative colitis: Golimumab (anti-TNF), infliximab (anti-TNF), budesonide (topical corticosteroid), tofacitinib (JAK inhibitor). All are approved for moderate-to-severe UC.
deepseek_explanations.psoriatic arthritis: Golimumab and infliximab (anti-TNF), apremilast (PDE4 inhibitor), leflunomide (DMARD) are all established treatments for psoriatic arthritis.
deepseek_explanations.non-hodgkin lymphoma: Doxorubicin and cyclophosphamide are part of CHOP regimen, appropriate. Methotrexate is used in some subtypes but not all. Pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1) is not first-line for most NHL except specific subtypes.
deepseek_explanations.allergic conjunctivitis: Cetirizine (oral antihistamine), olopatadine and levocabastine (topical antihistamines/mast cell stabilizers), lodoxamide (mast cell stabilizer). All are used for allergic conjunctivitis.
deepseek_explanations.pain: All four are NSAIDs, appropriate for mild-to-moderate acute or chronic pain. Not for severe or neuropathic pain, but the diagnosis 'pain' is broad and NSAIDs are reasonable first-line.
